preamble: The shift from the open-mid front vowel [ε] (the DRESS vowel) to the near-open front vowel [æ] (the TRAP vowel) is a hallmark of vowel lowering in several modern English chain shifts. As the historical [æ] vowel moves back or down, it creates a 'pull chain' that draws the [ε] vowel down into its vacated space.

examples.note: The DRESS vowel lowers significantly as part of the Canadian Shift.
Similar lowering dynamics occur in the California Vowel Shift before non-nasal consonants.
